mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0% signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where UNRATE crosses 6.0%, or signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series diverges, or combined Rank IC drops below 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: Signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance in expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 230.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation adaptively, yielding positive returns in >60% of high-CPI periods, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings growth for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when wage growth outpaces productivity, or interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue generation, directly translating to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDP revisions erase predictive power, resulting in Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling windows, or forward returns show no directional bias relative to GDP levels.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95.0 signals severe supply-side contraction and manufacturing recession, triggering risk-off capital flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Markets anticipate supply contractions in advance, causing the signal to lose predictive power with positive returns in >55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains negative.
